column3: DiagConfig: Configuration Diagnosis of Performance Violations in Configurable Software Systems
column10: The paper explicitly addresses post-deployment, operational diagnosis of SLO-violating performance incidents in running systems: it combines runtime profiling with static taint tracking to analyze real system configuration options and call-chains, and it names stakeholders (users, developers, and site reliability engineers) as actors. The artifact focus is on operational config files/options and live hotspots in deployed Java applications (e.g., Cassandra, H2) rather than design-time modeling.
column11: It presents DiagConfig, a white-box tool that identifies performance-sensitive configuration options, localizes performance-violating hotspot functions in running systems via profiling, and constructs cause鈥揺ffect chains linking runtime-config options to performance degradations to explain and guide configuration tuning.
column12: This work diagnoses configuration-induced failures after deployment by locating root-cause options responsible for SLO violations. It directly addresses operational misconfiguration troubleshooting and is therefore within our scope.
column21: Zhiming Chen; Pengfei Chen; Peipei Wang; Guangba Yu; Zilong He; Genting Mai

column3: White-box performance-influence models: A profiling and learning approach
column10: This work models configuration-dependent performance behavior at the method level to help developers locate performance bottlenecks.
column16: 2021 IEEE/ACM 43rd International Conference on Software Engineering
column21: Weber, Max, Sven Apel, and Norbert Siegmund
